taxonomy: Callista chrysantha belongs to the family of flowers known as Orchidaceae. It is classified as a type of orchid, specifically within the subfamily Orchidoideae. This classification is based on its unique flower structure and growth habits. Within the Orchidoideae subfamily, Callista chrysantha is placed in the genus Callista, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. This classification helps identify and distinguish Callista chrysantha from other orchid species.
uses: Callista chrysantha is known for its traditional medicinal uses in various cultures, particularly in Asia. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin conditions. Its roots and rhizomes are used in herbal remedies to alleviate fever and cough, as well as to treat digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Callista chrysantha is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Callista chrysantha as a medicinal plant.

constituents: Callista chrysantha, also known as the Golden Rain Tree, contains a range of active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds.
